title: Heatwave in Telangana with temperatures above 40°C - The News Mill
full_text:
Telangana is experiencing intense heatwave conditions with maximum temperatures exceeding 40°C in several areas over the past two days, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD) Scientist Dharmaraju. He stated that dry weather conditions are prevailing across the state and temperatures are expected to increase further in the coming days.
“For the past two days, Telangana State has been experiencing maximum temperatures above 40°C. Dry weather conditions are currently prevailing over the state,” Dharmaraju said.
He noted that several districts have recorded high temperatures, including Adilabad at around 41°C and Nizamabad at 41.4°C. Cities such as Medak, Hanumakonda, Khammam, and Mahabubnagar also reported temperatures near or above 40°C. “The city recorded a maximum temperature of 40.1°C yesterday,” he added, warning that temperatures may rise by 2°C to 3°C over the next five days.
The IMD morning bulletin predicted changing weather patterns across various parts of the country. A hot and humid weather warning was issued for multiple regions, including isolated pockets of Telangana, Rayalaseema, Madhya Maharashtra, North Interior Karnataka, Marathwada, Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, West Rajasthan, and Jharkhand between April 15 and 20, with differing peak periods.
The IMD also forecast likely hot and humid conditions in isolated areas of Gangetic West Bengal and Odisha during April 15-20, and in coastal Maharashtra, coastal Gujarat, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, coastal Andhra Pradesh, and coastal Karnataka during intervals between April 15 and 18. Authorities advised the public to remain cautious amid rising temperatures and uncomfortable weather.
In Northeast India, light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds is expected over Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, and Tripura between April 15 and 18, with heavy rainfall in some locations during this time.
Northwest India is forecast to receive rainfall and snowfall in Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, and Haryana, along with thunderstorms, strong winds, and hailstorms in certain areas of Jammu and Kashmir.
East India anticipates scattered rainfall with thunderstorms in West Bengal, Sikkim, and Odisha at various times from April 15 to 20. In South India, light to moderate rain with thunderstorms and gusty winds is expected over Interior and Coastal Karnataka between April 15 and 18.
The IMD advised people to remain alert as weather conditions may vary across regions.
